title: TC001-Run live sample and view analyzed result panels
description: Verifies a user can analyze the live sample and reaches /result with both original Marathi and canonical English panels displayed.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Click the 'Try live sample' button to load the sample into the input, then submit the sample for analysis by clicking 'Analyze your document'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section[2]/article/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/section/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Paste Marathi text and view side-by-side analysis result
description: Verifies a user can paste Marathi text, submit analysis, and see original Marathi and canonical English on /result.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Fill the Marathi text into the paste textarea (index 2) and click the Analyze button (index 71). Stop after the click and wait for the app to navigate to the result page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section[2]/section/div/div[2]/div[2]/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('सदर अधिसूचनेन्वये जिल्हा परिषद शाळांमधील इयत्ता पहिली ते आठवीच्या विद्यार्थ्यांसाठी शैक्षणिक साहित्य वितरणासाठी अर्ज दिनांक २५ एप्रिल २०२६ पर्यंत तालुका शिक्षण अधिकाऱ्याकडे सादर करावा. अर्जासोबत विद्यार्थी नोंद, शाळेचा शिक्का आणि मुख्याध्यापकांची स्वाक्षरी जोडणे आवश्यक आहे.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section[2]/section/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Analyze uploaded Marathi PDF and verify extracted Marathi is non-empty
description: Verifies a user can upload a Marathi PDF, run analysis, and see a non-empty Marathi source alongside canonical English on /result.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/result' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to /result after submitting the analysis request"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'मराठी')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The Marathi source should be visible after analysis so the original Marathi text is shown alongside the canonical English"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC004-Request plain-language explanation and key actions independently and append results
description: Verifies a user can request a plain explanation and key actions separately on /result and both outputs appear without overwriting each other.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Click the 'Try live sample' entry point to load the sample into the demo (element index 52), then wait for the app to respond.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section[2]/article/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Generate summary' button (element index 305) to request the plain-language explanation, then request action items separately and verify both outputs appear without overwriting each other.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section[2]/div/div[2]/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section[2]/div/div[2]/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC017-Show validation when submitting analysis with no input
description: Verifies submitting analysis without pasted text or a valid PDF shows a client-side validation error and does not proceed to results.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/")
        
        # -> Click the 'Analyze your document' button (index 71) with no PDF uploaded and no text in the textarea, then check for a client-side validation error and ensure the user remains on the landing page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div/section[2]/section/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
